name: Tawk.to API
what_it_does: Tawk.to REST API — free live chat and customer support platform REST API for accessing chat transcripts, visitor information, contacts, and agent management, enabling agents to automate customer support workflows and sync chat data with CRM systems.
use_cases: Agents syncing Tawk.to chat transcripts to CRM after conversations end via webhook and REST API
Lead capture — agents extracting visitor contact information from Tawk.to chats and creating CRM records
Support analytics — agents querying Tawk.to for chat volume, response times, and agent performance metrics
Contact management — agents updating Tawk.to contact properties with enriched data from CRM or external sources
Automated messaging — agents using Tawk.to API to send pre-chat messages or update chat status based on business hours
not_for: High-volume enterprise support — Tawk.to is free but limited in enterprise features; use Zendesk or Freshdesk for complex support workflows
Custom chatbot flows — Tawk.to's chatbot features are basic; use Intercom or Drift for sophisticated conversational automation
Programmatic chat initiation — Tawk.to API is primarily for data access, not initiating conversations
best_when: You need a completely free live chat widget with API access for basic CRM integration and support data export without any per-seat costs.
avoid_when: You need sophisticated chatbot automation, advanced routing, or enterprise SLA features — Tawk.to's free model limits advanced support automation.

auth.notes: API key from Tawk.to account administration. Passed as Bearer token. Single API key per account. No scope granularity. Webhooks use a separate webhook secret for signature verification.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free — unlimited agents, unlimited chats, no message limits
pricing.paid_tiers: Hire a Virtual Agent: $1/hour for managed agents. Remove Tawk.to branding: $19/month. No mandatory paid tiers.
pricing.notes: Tawk.to is completely free with unlimited usage. Revenue comes from optional services (virtual agent hire, white labeling). No API cost. Genuinely free with no credit card required.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; API calls appear rate-limited at ~60 req/min

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Basic REST API with limited documentation. Error responses include message field but lack detail. API covers chats, contacts, and properties. Webhook-first design for real-time events. Limited endpoint coverage compared to paid chat platforms.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Contact updates by email are idempotent. Chat transcript retrieval is idempotent. No idempotency keys for create operations.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits are not documented — agents must implement conservative request pacing and handle 429 responses
API coverage is limited compared to UI features — not all Tawk.to features are accessible via API
Webhook payloads require signature verification — agents must validate the X-tawk-signature header
Chat transcripts may be delayed in API — real-time events require webhooks rather than polling
Property types must be created in UI before agents can set custom property values via API

security_score_components.security_notes: GDPR compliant with DPA available. TLS enforced. No SOC2. API key with no scope granularity. Webhook signature verification available but implementation details sparse. Global infrastructure.
